name: The Records Office
description: Afternoon light comes through the blinds in flat bars. The office smells of toner and old paper. Somewhere down the corridor a club is tuning instruments, and the ordinariness of it is unbearable.

entities.name: Arakawa Shiori
entities.description: The records clerk. She listens with her whole face, and she will not bend the archive by a single character.
entities.nameMeaning: 荒川 (wild river) + 栞 (bookmark) — she keeps the place in the records while the current runs on regardless. Kind, and immovable.

lines.text: She looks up, and her expression is the one adults use for a student who has come to the wrong window.
Nemoto-kun, isn't it? You asked about this on Monday too.
There's no Tōyama in the second-year rolls. There's never been a Tōyama at this school.

choices.label: Ask her to pull the enrolment book itself, not the terminal.
choices.reply: She sighs, but she goes and gets it. Paper, not screen.

success.text: The page for her class has thirty-one names and thirty-two desks in the seating diagram opposite. The mismatch is nothing. It is also the first nothing that has ever been on your side.

failure.text: Thirty-one names, thirty-one desks. You read it twice. The second time is worse, because you can feel yourself wanting the numbers to be wrong.

choices.label: Tell her plainly: someone is missing, and you are the only one who remembers her.
choices.reply: You say it out loud, in a school office, in the afternoon.

success.text: She does not believe you. But she does not laugh, and she does not reach for the phone to call anyone about you. She says: come back on Thursday, when I'm on the desk alone.

failure.text: Her face closes, gently, the way a door closes when someone is being careful not to slam it. She asks whether you've been sleeping. She means it kindly. That is the part that lands.

choices.label: Apologise for taking her time, and go.
choices.reply: "Sorry. I know you're busy." It costs something to say it politely.

entities.name: enrolment ledger
entities.description: A hardbound book of admissions, spine cracked from twenty years of being right.

lines.text: You do not need to open it to know what it says. You open it anyway.

entities.name: class photograph
entities.description: Last year's second-years on the gymnasium steps, framed and slightly crooked.

lines.text: Third row, fourth from the left. You know exactly where she stood, because you were the one who told her to stop hiding behind Kurosawa.
There is a girl there. She has Suzune's posture and someone else's face, and the caption gives her a name you have never heard.

interaction.afterText: You have looked at it once. You are not going to look at it again today.

transition.hint: The corridor outside the records office, mid-afternoon. Where Itsuki goes next depends on what he just learned; if he has the seating diagram he has somewhere to take it.
